figure.title: Blocked Responses and Watchdog Expiration
figure.purpose: Illustrates how a link failure blocks responses from ACC A to ACC B, leading to watchdog timer expiration in ACC B.

technical_summary: A failure is shown on the link between ACC A and the Switch.
A request path from ACC B to ACC A is traced, but responses back to ACC B are blocked at multiple points (marked with 'X').
As a result of the blocked responses, watchdog timers in ACC B's originator (UADP/UPU Originator) will eventually expire.

components.name: Watchdog Timer
components.description: Timer in ACC B's originator that detects missing responses.

events.name: Watchdog Expiration
events.description: Timer expires due to missing responses.

critical_facts: When a link fails, outstanding requests will time out, triggering watchdog timer expirations at the originating device.
